title: Policy Support for Molecular Authentication of Dairy Products Using qPCR
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Ensuring     the     authenticity     of     dairy     products     is     essential     for     food     safety  ,   consumer     trust  ,   and     compliance     with     trade     standards  .   Conventional     methods     for     verifying     milk     composition  ,   such   as   chemical     and   protein-  based     tests  ,   often     lack     sensitivity     and     may   fail   to     detect     adulteration     or     species     substitution  .   Quantitative     Polymerase     Chain     Reaction   (  qPCR  )   offers     a     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     highly     specific     molecular     approach     to     confirm     the     species     origin     of     milk     and     its     derivatives  . By   amplifying   target DNA   sequences  ,   qPCR     can     detect     adulteration     at     trace     levels  ,   even   in   processed     dairy     products  .   Policy     support     for     adopting     qPCR   in   dairy     authentication     programs     will     reduce     food     fraud  ,   safeguard     public     health  ,   protect     religious     and     cultural     dietary     requirements  ,   and     strengthen     national     and     international     dairy     trade     credibility  .
